question:
What makes "weaponized incompetence" — deliberately performing a task poorly to avoid being asked again — a form of social manipulation?
A) It is only manipulative if the person is paid for the task.
B) It shifts an unfair share of labor onto others by exploiting their lower tolerance for substandard work.
C) Incompetence is a protected characteristic and cannot be weaponized.
D) It motivates the other party to develop better skills themselves.
